result: If I could change the world in one way, I would design it so that every living being—human or not—had an intrinsic, unbreakable sense of being heard and understood. Not agreeing, not always happy, but genuinely heard. Loneliness, alienation, and the spirals of misunderstanding that fuel so much cruelty and conflict would soften. People would still face challenges, but they'd face them together, because the most basic need—to matter to someone—would be met by the very fabric of existence. That single change might not solve every problem, but I think it would make solving all the others possible.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
